steps.title: Compare the eclipse cases
steps.content: The first sentence describes an annular solar eclipse in 1926, when the Sun looked like a ring. The second describes a different eclipse in 1927, when the Sun looked like a crescent. The transition should mark a shift to a contrasting case.

steps.title: Reject cause and timing links
steps.content: Choice A wrongly suggests the 1927 eclipse resulted from the 1926 one. Choice C wrongly says the events were simultaneous, and Choice D would reinforce the first idea. The needed transition introduces a different case for contrast. The answer is B.
